summary:
Claim: The Gleason score accurately reflects histopathological features of prostate cancer.  
Evidence: - The Gleason score evaluates glandular architecture and cellular differentiation, assigning values from 2 to 10 based on malignancy grade. - Prostate cancer arises predominantly in the peripheral zone, not the central zone, though this does not invalidate the Gleason scoring system.  
Answer: A  

(487 tokens)  

Note: Despite correcting the anatomical origin of prostate cancer, the core scientific validity of the Gleason score—its direct link to histopathological assessment—remains unchallenged. Thus, option A stands as both factually and logically consistent across revisions. Options B, C, and D contain inaccuracies related to tumor location, histotype prevalence, or hormonal dependence. Final choice reaffirms A as the only fully supported claim.
